meta.originalTitle: A survivor’s story 新西兰猫头鹰

children.text: You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 1–13, which are based on Reading Passage 1 below.
One native bird in New Zealand that has managed to survive the introduction of non-native species

As an island country with a fauna dominated by birds, New Zealand was once home to an owl species which is now
        extinct, the laughing owl, named for its distinctive cry. This bird was widespread throughout the islands when
        European settlers arrived in the middle of the 19th century and it remained in good numbers for some years
        thereafter. Where other native birds suffered from predation by the Polynesian rat, the laughing owl turned the
        tables and adapted its diet to include the rodent. It was also capable of catching and killing the other New
        Zealand owl, the morepork, and even larger birds, such as the weka. However, the laughing owl was wiped out
        around the beginning of the 20th century, its demise caused by specimen collectors, habitat changes, and
        non-native predators including cats and stoats. Surprisingly, it is the smaller owl, the morepork, that has
        managed to survive until this day.

Speckled dark brown, with yellow eyes and long tails, they are around 29 centimetres long from head to tail and
        175 grams in weight. Moreporks have fringes on the edge of their feathers, so they can fly almost silently and
        not alert potential prey. They have acute hearing and their large eyes are very sensitive to light.

Moreporks nest in tree hollows, in clumps of plants, or in cavities among rocks and roots. In the wild,
        moreporks usually start nesting in October, although two specimens have been recorded nesting in mid-winter,
        possibly stimulated by an ample food supply. The female lays up to three white eggs, which she incubates for 20
        to 30 days. During this time, she rarely hunts, and the male brings food to her.

Once the chicks hatch, she stays mainly on the nest until the young owls are fully feathered. When hatched,
        chicks are covered in light grey down and have their eyes closed. The eyes do not open until the eighth day
        after hatching. They can fly at around 35 days.

By day, moreporks sleep in roosts. By night, they hunt a variety of animals – mainly large invertebrates
        including scarab and huhu beetles, moths, caterpillars and spiders. They also take small birds and mice. They
        can find suitable food in pine forest as well as native forest. A morepork uses its sharp talons to catch or
        stun its prey, which it then carries away in its bill. Moreporks are clever hunters, and birds such as robins,
        grey warblers and fantails can end up as their prey. In the day, these small birds sometimes mob drowsy
        moreporks and chase them away from their roosts; they force the sleepy predators to search for a more peaceful
        spot.

Moreporks have proved to be ungracious hosts. Scientists trying to establish a population of plovers on Motuora
        Island in New Zealand's Hauraki Gulf were mystified as to why only two birds survived out of the 75 placed
        there. The culprits turned out to be five pairs of moreporks that ate or chased away the new arrivals.

Although moreporks are still considered to be relatively common, it is likely that numbers are in gradual
        decline due to predation and loss of habitat. As the female is a hole-nester, she is vulnerable to predators
        such as stoats and possums during the breeding season, and eggs and chicks will also be at risk from rats. The
        use of pesticides is another possible threat to the owls, though not a direct one. As moreporks are at the top
        of the food chain, they could be affected by an accumulative poison by consuming prey that has ingested poison.

The New Zealand Department of Conservation is taking steps to ensure the preservation of New Zealand's only
        native owl. The department is involved in measuring the population of moreporks and has put transmitters on a
        number of birds to determine survival and mortality. As well as being New Zealand's only native owl, the
        morepork has symbolic and spiritual importance, so in monitoring the birds it is hoped that the morepork will
        continue to survive and thrive.

At dusk, the melancholy sound of the morepork can be heard in forests and parks as it calls to other moreporks
        and claims territory. Its Māori name (ruru) echoes its two-part cry. In the tradition of the Māori people of New
        Zealand, the morepork, or ruru, was often seen as a careful guardian. A number of sayings referred to the bird's
        alertness. As a bird of the night, it was associated with the spirit world. Moreporks were believed to act as
        messengers to the gods in the heavens, flying along spiritual paths in the sky. They were the mediums used to
        communicate with the gods. The occasional high, piercing call of the morepork signified bad news, but the
        lower-pitched and more common “ruru” call heralded good news.
Do the following statements agree with the information in Reading Passage 1?
In boxes 1–7 on your answer sheet, write:
if the statement agrees with the information
if the statement contradicts the information
if there is no information about this
Early European settlers made detailed studies of the morepork.
The Polynesian rat had a negative effect on the number of laughing owls.
The laughing owl was larger than the morepork.
Rats pose a risk to young moreporks.

The New Zealand Department of Conservation is hoping to limit the population of
            moreporks.
Other bird species are frightened away when they hear the morepork's cry.
In Māori tradition, the low call of the morepork had negative associations.

explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：早期欧洲殖民者对莫波克鸮进行了详细研究。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：定位文章 Paragraph 1 中 “European settlers arrived...in the middle of the 19th century”，文中仅提到 19 世纪中叶欧洲殖民者抵达新西兰时笑鸮的生存状况，以及莫波克鸮后来存活下来，但未提及欧洲殖民者是否对莫波克鸮做过 “详细研究”，既无肯定信息，也无否定信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。
题目翻译：波利尼西亚鼠对笑鸮的数量产生了负面影响。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “Where other native birds suffered from predation by the Polynesian rat, the laughing owl turned the tables and adapted its diet to include the rodent”，可知波利尼西亚鼠会捕食其他本土鸟类，但笑鸮反而将这种老鼠纳入自己的食谱，波利尼西亚鼠并未 “减少” 笑鸮的数量，与题干中 “产生负面影响” 的描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。
题目翻译：笑鸮的体型比莫波克鸮大。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “Surprisingly, it is the smaller owl, the morepork, that has managed to survive until this day”，明确指出莫波克鸮是 “体型更小的猫头鹰”，由此可推断笑鸮的体型比莫波克鸮大，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。
题目翻译：老鼠对莫波克鸮幼鸟构成威胁。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 7 中 “eggs and chicks will also be at risk from rats”，直接说明老鼠会对莫波克鸮的鸟卵和雏鸟（即 “幼鸟”）构成威胁，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。
题目翻译：新西兰自然资源保护部希望限制莫波克鸮的种群数量。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 8 中 “The New Zealand Department of Conservation is taking steps to ensure the preservation of New Zealand’s only native owl”，可知保护部门的目的是 “保护” 莫波克鸮，而非 “限制” 其种群数量，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。
题目翻译：其他鸟类听到莫波克鸮的叫声后会被吓跑。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “In the day, these small birds sometimes mob drowsy moreporks and chase them away from their roosts”，文中仅提到白天小型鸟类会聚集起来骚扰并驱赶莫波克鸮，但未提及这些鸟类是 “听到莫波克鸮的叫声后被吓跑”，也无其他段落涉及 “叫声对其他鸟类的威慑作用”，信息缺失，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。
题目翻译：在毛利人的传统中，莫波克鸮的低沉叫声带有负面含义。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 9 中 “the lower-pitched and more common ‘ruru’ call heralded good news”，可知在毛利传统中，莫波克鸮低沉的叫声预示 “好消息”，而非题干中所说的 “带有负面含义”，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

items.text:
（1）题目 1：Early European settlers made detailed studies of the morepork.
题目翻译：早期欧洲殖民者对莫波克鸮进行了详细研究。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：定位文章 Paragraph 1 中 “European settlers arrived...in the middle of the 19th century”，文中仅提到 19 世纪中叶欧洲殖民者抵达新西兰时笑鸮的生存状况，以及莫波克鸮后来存活下来，但未提及欧洲殖民者是否对莫波克鸮做过 “详细研究”，既无肯定信息，也无否定信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。

（2）题目 2：The Polynesian rat had a negative effect on the number of laughing owls.
题目翻译：波利尼西亚鼠对笑鸮的数量产生了负面影响。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “Where other native birds suffered from predation by the Polynesian rat, the laughing owl turned the tables and adapted its diet to include the rodent”，可知波利尼西亚鼠会捕食其他本土鸟类，但笑鸮反而将这种老鼠纳入自己的食谱，波利尼西亚鼠并未 “减少” 笑鸮的数量，与题干中 “产生负面影响” 的描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

（3）题目 3：The laughing owl was larger than the morepork.
题目翻译：笑鸮的体型比莫波克鸮大。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “Surprisingly, it is the smaller owl, the morepork, that has managed to survive until this day”，明确指出莫波克鸮是 “体型更小的猫头鹰”，由此可推断笑鸮的体型比莫波克鸮大，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

（4）题目 4：Rats pose a risk to young moreporks.
题目翻译：老鼠对莫波克鸮幼鸟构成威胁。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 7 中 “eggs and chicks will also be at risk from rats”，直接说明老鼠会对莫波克鸮的鸟卵和雏鸟（即 “幼鸟”）构成威胁，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

（5）题目 5：The New Zealand Department of Conservation is hoping to limit the population of moreporks.
题目翻译：新西兰自然资源保护部希望限制莫波克鸮的种群数量。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 8 中 “The New Zealand Department of Conservation is taking steps to ensure the preservation of New Zealand’s only native owl”，可知保护部门的目的是 “保护” 莫波克鸮，而非 “限制” 其种群数量，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

（6）题目 6：Other bird species are frightened away when they hear the morepork’s cry.
题目翻译：其他鸟类听到莫波克鸮的叫声后会被吓跑。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “In the day, these small birds sometimes mob drowsy moreporks and chase them away from their roosts”，文中仅提到白天小型鸟类会聚集起来骚扰并驱赶莫波克鸮，但未提及这些鸟类是 “听到莫波克鸮的叫声后被吓跑”，也无其他段落涉及 “叫声对其他鸟类的威慑作用”，信息缺失，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。

（7）题目 7：In Māori tradition, the low call of the morepork had negative associations.
题目翻译：在毛利人的传统中，莫波克鸮的低沉叫声带有负面含义。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 9 中 “the lower-pitched and more common ‘ruru’ call heralded good news”，可知在毛利传统中，莫波克鸮低沉的叫声预示 “好消息”，而非题干中所说的 “带有负面含义”，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

children.text: from the passage for each answer.
feathers with fringes to enable quiet flight
nests in trees, plants or spaces in roots and
after about 35 days, baby moreporks are capable of leaving the nest
can be chased away by other birds during the

that had been introduced to Motuora
                  Island

explanationSection.sectionTitle: 2. 笔记填空题（Questions 8–13：ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER）
explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：外观 —— 体长约_\___\__
答案：29 centimetres（29 厘米）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “they are around 29 centimetres long from head to tail”，明确提到莫波克鸮从头部到尾部的长度约为 29 厘米，直接提取 “29 centimetres”，符合题干 “体长约……” 的要求。
题目翻译：筑巢 —— 在树上、植物丛中，或在树根与_\___\__之间的缝隙中筑巢
答案：rocks（岩石）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “in cavities among rocks and roots”，可知莫波克鸮会在岩石与树根之间的缝隙中筑巢，题干中已提及 “roots（树根）”，因此补充 “rocks（岩石）”。
题目翻译：捕猎 —— 用它的_\___\__运送猎物
答案：bill（喙）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “which it then carries away in its bill”，可知莫波克鸮用喙（bill）叼走猎物，即 “通过喙运输猎物”，符合题干要求，因此填 “bill”。
题目翻译：捕猎 —— 在_\___\__会被其他鸟类赶走
答案：day（白天）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “In the day, these small birds sometimes mob drowsy moreporks and chase them away from their roosts”，可知其他小型鸟类在 “白天” 骚扰并驱赶莫波克鸮，因此填 “day”。
题目翻译：捕猎 —— 攻击了被引入莫图奥拉岛的_\___\__
答案：plovers（珩科鸟）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “Scientists trying to establish a population of plovers on Motuora Island... The culprits turned out to be five pairs of moreporks that ate or chased away the new arrivals”，可知 “新来者” 指被引入莫图奥拉岛的珩科鸟（plovers），莫波克鸮攻击了这些珩科鸟，因此填 “plovers”。
题目翻译：威胁 —— 可能通过猎物接触到_\___\__
答案：poison（毒素）
解析：定位 Paragraph 7 中 “they could be affected by an accumulative poison by consuming prey that has ingested poison”，可知莫波克鸮可能通过食用体内含有毒素的猎物，间接摄入 “毒素（poison）”，符合题干 “通过猎物接触到……” 的要求，因此填 “poison”。

items.text:
（1）题目 8：Appearance - approximately \___\___ in length
题目翻译：外观 —— 体长约_\___\__
答案：29 centimetres（29 厘米）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “they are around 29 centimetres long from head to tail”，明确提到莫波克鸮从头部到尾部的长度约为 29 厘米，直接提取 “29 centimetres”，符合题干 “体长约……” 的要求。

（2）题目 9：Nesting - nests in trees, plants or spaces in roots and \___\___
题目翻译：筑巢 —— 在树上、植物丛中，或在树根与_\___\__之间的缝隙中筑巢
答案：rocks（岩石）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “in cavities among rocks and roots”，可知莫波克鸮会在岩石与树根之间的缝隙中筑巢，题干中已提及 “roots（树根）”，因此补充 “rocks（岩石）”。

（3）题目 10：Hunting - transports its prey using its \___\___
题目翻译：捕猎 —— 用它的_\___\__运送猎物
答案：bill（喙）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “which it then carries away in its bill”，可知莫波克鸮用喙（bill）叼走猎物，即 “通过喙运输猎物”，符合题干要求，因此填 “bill”。

（4）题目 11：Hunting - can be chased away by other birds during the \___\___
题目翻译：捕猎 —— 在_\___\__会被其他鸟类赶走
答案：day（白天）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “In the day, these small birds sometimes mob drowsy moreporks and chase them away from their roosts”，可知其他小型鸟类在 “白天” 骚扰并驱赶莫波克鸮，因此填 “day”。

（5）题目 12：Hunting - attacked \___\___ that had been introduced to Motuora Island
题目翻译：捕猎 —— 攻击了被引入莫图奥拉岛的_\___\__
答案：plovers（珩科鸟）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “Scientists trying to establish a population of plovers on Motuora Island... The culprits turned out to be five pairs of moreporks that ate or chased away the new arrivals”，可知 “新来者” 指被引入莫图奥拉岛的珩科鸟（plovers），莫波克鸮攻击了这些珩科鸟，因此填 “plovers”。

（6）题目 13：Threats - may be exposed to \___\___ in their prey
题目翻译：威胁 —— 可能通过猎物接触到_\___\__
答案：poison（毒素）
解析：定位 Paragraph 7 中 “they could be affected by an accumulative poison by consuming prey that has ingested poison”，可知莫波克鸮可能通过食用体内含有毒素的猎物，间接摄入 “毒素（poison）”，符合题干 “通过猎物接触到……” 的要求，因此填 “poison”。
